channel.title: Salmon HVAC Blog
channel.description: HVAC tips, rebate guides, repair advice, and equipment resources for Utah homeowners from Salmon HVAC.

item.title: AC Emergency in a Utah Heat Wave: What to Do When Your AC Quits at 100°F
item.description: Your AC quit during a Utah heat wave? A step-by-step guide to the safe checks that can restore cooling, how to keep your family safe in the heat, and when to call for emergency AC repair.

item.title: Ductless Mini-Split Systems in Utah: Costs, Best Uses & Whether They're Worth It
item.description: A Utah homeowner's guide to ductless mini-splits: how they work, 2026 installed costs, cold-climate winter performance, rebates, and when they beat central air.

item.title: Commercial Rooftop Unit (RTU) Repair vs. Replacement in Northern Utah: Costs, Signs & Lifespan
item.description: When should a Northern Utah business replace a commercial rooftop HVAC unit instead of repairing it? RTU lifespan, real replacement cost ranges, the 50% repair rule, refrigerant transition issues, and the warning signs that replacement is near.

item.title: Outside AC Fan Not Spinning in Utah? Capacitor, Breaker, Motor, and Fix-It-First Repair Guide
item.description: Outside AC fan humming but not spinning? Learn the likely causes, typical capacitor and fan motor repair costs, safe homeowner checks, and how Salmon HVAC fixes your current unit first whenever possible.

item.title: Wildfire Smoke & Your HVAC System in Utah: Filters, Fan Settings, and Clean Indoor Air
item.description: Should you run your AC during Utah wildfire smoke? Learn the right HVAC fan settings, MERV 13 filter guidance, clean-room setup, and when to call Salmon HVAC.

item.title: AC Repair in Salt Lake City, UT: Costs, Common Problems & What to Expect
item.description: AC not cooling in Salt Lake City? Real repair costs, the most common SLC AC failures — capacitors, refrigerant leaks, frozen coils, and cottonwood-clogged condensers — and what to expect from a same-day service call.

item.title: HVAC Replacement Cost in Utah: 2026 Pricing Guide
item.description: How much does HVAC replacement cost in Utah in 2026? See realistic AC, furnace, heat pump, dual-fuel, ductwork, rebate, and financing factors for northern Utah homes.

item.title: HVAC for Room Additions, Finished Basements & ADUs in Utah
item.description: Adding a room addition, finishing a basement, or building an ADU in northern Utah? Your existing HVAC system probably can't handle it. Here's what you need to know — and why ductless mini-splits are usually the right answer.

item.title: R-410A Refrigerant Phaseout: What Utah Homeowners Need to Know in 2026
item.description: New AC equipment made after January 2025 can no longer use R-410A. Here's what this means for repairs, replacement costs, and your options if your Utah home has an existing R-410A system.

item.title: Mild April in Utah? Why Now Is the Best Time to Get Ahead of AC Season
item.description: A mild April can make AC service feel optional. Learn why late April is the best time for Utah homeowners to test, clean, and schedule AC service before the first heat wave.

item.title: How Long Does an AC Unit Last in Utah? The Repair-vs-Replace Tipping Point
item.description: Utah's climate shortens AC lifespan to 12–16 years. Learn the $5,000 Rule, Utah-specific warning signs, and when replacement makes more sense than another repair.

item.title: HVAC Zoning Systems for Utah Homes: Costs, Benefits, and ROI
item.description: Hot upstairs bedrooms and cold basements are common in northern Utah. Learn what HVAC zoning costs, who benefits most, and how zone control can improve comfort and efficiency.

item.title: Spring AC Tune-Up Checklist for Utah Homeowners: What to Do Before Summer
item.description: Northern Utah's first 90°F day can arrive before Memorial Day. Here's what a professional spring tune-up covers, what you can do yourself, and what we find most often after a Utah winter.

item.title: Why Is My Utah Home So Dry In Winter?
item.description: Utah winter air is among the driest in the country. Learn why your home loses humidity, the signs of a dry air problem, and how a whole-home HVAC humidifier solves it permanently.

item.title: Utah Heat Pump Rebates & Tax Credits: The Complete 2026 Guide
item.description: Stack Rocky Mountain Power Wattsmart rebates, Dominion Energy ThermWise incentives, and federal IRA tax credits to save thousands on a heat pump installation.

item.title: The Complete Guide to VRF Systems for Utah Commercial Buildings
item.description: Everything Utah building owners need to know about Variable Refrigerant Flow systems — how they work, ROI, cold-climate performance, and which buildings benefit most.

item.title: Do Heat Pumps Work in Utah's Cold Winters?
item.description: Modern cold-climate heat pumps operate to -13°F. We break down how they perform across Utah's climate zones and when a dual-fuel system makes more sense.

item.title: Utah Heat Pump Rebates & Tax Credits: The Complete 2026 Guide for Homeowners
item.description: Utah homeowners can still claim thousands in heat pump savings through Rocky Mountain Power Wattsmart and Enbridge Gas ThermWise rebates — even after the federal 25C credit expired. Here's exactly how to stack every available incentive in 2026.

item.title: Utah HVAC Air Filter Replacement Guide
item.description: Did you know that Utah air quality can impact how long your HVAC air filter lasts?

item.title: Air Conditioner Not Cooling? Top Causes and Fixes for Utah Homes
item.description: If your air conditioner isn’t cooling your Utah home, you’re not alone. From low refrigerant to clogged filters and frozen coils, there are several common issues that can cause AC failure—especially in Northern Utah’s dry, dusty climate. In this guide, Salmon HVAC breaks down the top causes and what you can do to fix them before calling in the pros.

item.title: Why Regular HVAC Maintenance Matters More in Utah’s Dry Climate
item.description: Utah’s dry, dusty climate puts unique strain on HVAC systems, making regular maintenance essential—not optional. From brittle components to clogged ductwork, neglecting routine service can lead to higher energy bills, poor air quality, and costly repairs. In this guide, Salmon HVAC explains why Utah homeowners need a customized maintenance approach to keep systems running efficiently year-round.

item.title: Indoor Air Quality in Utah: What Every Homeowner Needs to Know
item.description: Indoor air quality is a growing concern for Utah homeowners, especially with the state’s dry climate, wildfire smoke, and winter inversions. Learn how your HVAC system influences the air you breathe—and discover practical solutions from Salmon HVAC to improve comfort, health, and efficiency throughout your home.

Learn how to spot the top warning signs—like strange noises, inconsistent cooling, or rising energy bills—that indicate your AC may need immediate repair. item.title: Spring Into Comfort: Why Now Is the Time for Air Conditioning Service in Utah
item.description: As Utah transitions into spring, now is the perfect time to schedule your air conditioning service. Avoid summer breakdowns, lower your energy bills, and keep your home comfortable with a professional AC tune-up from Salmon HVAC. Learn why spring maintenance is essential for Utah homeowners and how it can save you money all season long.

item.title: What Indoor Air Quality Solutions Are Available from Utah HVAC Experts?
item.description: Discover how Utah homeowners can improve indoor air quality with expert HVAC solutions. From HEPA filters and UV purifiers to whole-home humidifiers and smart ventilation systems, learn how to create a healthier, more comfortable home environment year-round.

item.title: Why Is Regular HVAC Maintenance Important for Long-Term Efficiency in Utah?
item.description: Utah's dust, altitude, and temperature extremes make HVAC maintenance more critical than in most states. Learn what a tune-up includes and why the math strongly favors prevention.

item.title: How to Choose the Best HVAC Repair Service in Utah
item.description: Looking for the best HVAC repair service in Utah? Learn how to choose a reliable provider for your furnace and AC needs with expert tips on pricing, licensing, and customer reviews.

item.title: When Should You Call for Emergency HVAC Repair Services in Utah?
item.description: In Utah, recognizing when to seek emergency HVAC repair is crucial due to extreme weather challenges. Key indicators include unusual noises, lack of heating or cooling, and significant leaks. Immediate professional assistance is necessary to avoid severe damage and ensure safety. Regular maintenance can help prevent these emergencies.

item.title: What Are the Benefits of Energy-Efficient HVAC System Installation in Utah?
item.description: Investing in an energy-efficient HVAC system is highly beneficial for Utah homeowners, offering reduced energy consumption, lower utility costs, enhanced indoor air quality, and increased property value. These systems are designed to handle extreme temperatures and promote environmental sustainability while providing financial incentives through rebates and tax credits.

item.title: What Should You Look for in HVAC Maintenance Plans Offered by Salt Lake City HVAC Companies?
item.description: Choosing the right HVAC maintenance plan in Salt Lake City is essential for keeping your system running efficiently year-round. A quality plan should include regular inspections, system tune-ups, and filter replacements while offering priority service and repair discounts. With Utah’s extreme seasonal temperatures, investing in a maintenance plan can help prevent costly breakdowns, improve energy efficiency, and extend the lifespan of your HVAC system. Learn what to look for in a comprehensive plan to ensure comfort and reliability in your home.
